topic: A student preparing for an exam
text: The ink on the practice exam seemed to liquefy and swirl, the complex variables transforming into indecipherable thorns. My throat felt as though it were being constricted by an invisible hand, making each swallow a labored effort. I pulled my chair closer to the desk, but the shadows in the corner of the room appeared to creep forward with every flicker of the fluorescent light. My hands, cold and clammy, shook so violently that the pen slipped from my grip, clattering against the floor with a sound like a gunshot in the oppressive silence.

topic: A student preparing for an exam
text: Every time the radiator hissed, Julian flinched, his shoulders hunching toward his ears. He stared at the stack of textbooks, his pulse thundering in his temples with a frantic, uneven rhythm that drowned out the ticking clock. His stomach was a cold knot of lead, and a thin sheen of perspiration coated his upper lip despite the chill in the library. He wanted to look away from the page, but he felt as though breaking eye contact with the material would allow the looming failure to finally pounce from the darkness of his own mind.

topic: A student preparing for an exam
text: I sat paralyzed, the glow of the laptop screen casting a ghostly pallor over my features. I reached for a glass of water, but my fingers were numb, fumbling uselessly against the coaster. I couldn't shake the sensation that the walls were slowly tilting inward, threatening to crush me under the weight of everything I didn't know.

text: Elena’s eyes darted toward the door at the slightest creak of the floorboards. She felt a sharp, stinging prickle across her skin, as if the air itself had turned electric and hostile. Her heart felt less like an organ and more like a trapped animal slamming itself against the bars of her ribs.
